_comment: Source-of-truth manifest. filament.py slices each STL once and writes ../src/lib/data/parts.generated.json + thumbnails + STL copies. Per part: id, name, category, description (short, shown in app), internal_notes (extended, agent-only, NOT emitted to the app), version, created_at, updated_at, stl, quantity (per ONE instance of its category), color, optional. attributes (optional list of {label,value} shown in the app), versions (optional archetype history: list of {version,date,message,commit}; commit ties to a real git commit, null = pending an upcoming clean commit). low_tolerance (optional bool: part has little tolerance for dimensional error - a tight/precise fit - so a test print is worth doing) + low_tolerance_note (the specifics, shown in the app). color is one of {"role":"<roleid>"} (user-picked), {"fixed":"<lego-id>"}, {"split":[{"color":"<lego-id>","qty":N},...]} (sums to quantity), or {"any":true}, or {"by_section":{"<sectionid>":<colorspec>,...}} (per-section color when a part lives in multiple sections). Machine = 1 of each non-scaling category + N of each scaling category. See ../notes/TERMINOLOGY.md. SCHEMA V2 (unified parts system, see ../notes/UNIFIED-PARTS-SYSTEM.md, incremental): a part may carry kind ('printed' when absent | 'cots'); cots parts have cots {type,size?,variant?}, category (display grouping on the hardware tab), note (builder-critical caveat from the BOM sheet), image (slicer-relative path, uploaded to the bucket by sync_bucket.py and served from there) OR image_url (an already-uploaded content-addressed bucket URL; BOM product images never touch git), sourcing {vendors:[{region,vendor,url,affiliate_url?,price,currency?,pack_qty?,as_of?,note?}]} (url is always the plain listing; affiliate_url is the same listing carrying the project's Amazon tag, mirroring the BOM sheet's 'US Source 1 with Affiliate' column — both are kept so a builder can choose) (price is USD unless currency says otherwise), and no stl/color/quantities. sheet_qty {per_machine|per_layer} / sheet_qty_text are TRANSITIONAL hand counts carried over from the BOM sheet; they die once these parts are placed as requires/lines in the tree. A printed part may carry requires [{part,qty}] = hardware committed to that single physical part (heat inserts, press-fit bearings); qty scales with the part automatically. An assembly may carry lines [{part|assembly, qty}] (qty is a number, or 'per-layer' = multiplied by the total layer count, or 'middle-layers' = layer count minus 2, the layers that sit between the top and bottom interfaces) and status 'stub' (placeholder, nothing inside yet) or 'partial' (some lines filled in) — these form the experimental machine tree rooted at the 'machine' assembly. Line ids are unique within an assembly: two joints that use the same screw are one merged line. Screws and other hardware that JOIN parts are lines of the assembly that makes the joint, never requires of either member (requires is only for hardware committed to one physical part). joining [{method,note}] records how the lines become one unit — 'solder'|'crimp'|'glue'|'press'|'self-tap' (screw cuts its own thread in printed plastic, no insert or nut) |'friction' (held by clamping force alone, e.g. jammed in an extrusion slot). A cots part that is cut from a bought length carries stock {unit_length_mm, cut_length_mm, pieces_per_unit, unit_label, piece_label}; its lines count PIECES and the app divides to get lengths to buy.

sections.name: Electronics
sections.experimental_note: The electronics are early and changing a lot. Only build these if you have the experience to adapt as the design moves.

parts.name: Stator
parts.description: Feeder stator. 4 required: 1 gray + 3 black.
parts.internal_notes: One per C-channel area. 1 gray, 3 black. Required. ~240mm disc (bed-max).

parts.name: Rotor (faceted)
parts.description: Faceted rotor. 3 required: 2 white + 1 gray.
parts.internal_notes: 2 white, 1 gray. Required.

parts.name: Output gear (130T)
parts.description: Output gear, 130 teeth. One per C-channel (4 total).
parts.internal_notes: 1 per C-channel => 4 total. Feeder color. Takes the 6806 (30 mm bore) bearing.

parts.name: NEMA bracket
parts.description: NEMA motor bracket. One per C-channel (4 total).
parts.internal_notes: 1 per C-channel => 4 total. Spencer believes all NEMA brackets are the same across C-channels. Feeder color. v2 STL from 01_c_channel (5).zip.

parts.name: Light post
parts.description: Light post on the C-channel feeder. 2 total.
parts.internal_notes: Part of the feeder. 2x, feeder color. STL from 01_c_channel (5).zip.

parts.name: Light post cap
parts.description: Light post cap. 2 total (C-channels 2 & 3). White.
parts.internal_notes: 2 of the C-channels (2 and 3) have these. White. NOT the big classification dome.

parts.name: Light post cap adapter
parts.description: Adapts the light post cap to the C-channel. 2 per machine.
parts.internal_notes: Light post cap mount/adapter, C-channel feeder. Light post (feeder) colour.

parts.name: Bulk cap
parts.description: Bulk cap. Sits on top of the first C-channel.
parts.internal_notes: 1 bulk cap, on top of the first C-channel. Color not critical; default black (follows feeder color). File: 'Bulk Cap B'. Distinct from the bulk bucket (separate bed-max optional part, not yet provided).

parts.name: Classification dome
parts.description: Large classification-channel dome (~full bed). White.
parts.internal_notes: Big dome, almost the entire print bed. White (lighting/diffusion). Was previously mislabeled 'light cap'. QUANTITY ASSUMED 1 - confirm.

parts.name: Interface bracket
parts.description: Interface bracket. Pairs with the spacer; 6 on the interface.

parts.suspicious_note: The fit onto the interface spoke (long) is too tight — there's no clearance.

parts.name: Interface spacer
parts.description: Interface spacer. Pairs with the bracket; 6 on the interface.
parts.internal_notes: 6 on the interface. Frame color. File: 000_interface_layer_assembly - bottom_spacer.

parts.name: External bracket — side

parts.low_tolerance_note: A test print is suggested. This part is a tight fit around the 2020 aluminum profile, so print one first and confirm it seats properly before committing to the full set — dial in your printer and profile together.

parts.internal_notes: Replaces ext-bracket-bottom-vertical + ext-bracket-cover on the bottom-most distribution layer (6 per machine). Shorter so the extrusion pops out to mount the wheels.
parts.info: For the bottom-most layer only. It is shorter than the standard bottom vertical + cover so the extrusion can pop out and the wheels mount to it.

parts.name: M3 screw — type and length TBD
parts.description: Placeholder for the M3s whose head type and length nobody has written down yet: the cable-mount cage bracket, the camera extension, and the two board mounts.
parts.note: Neither the head type nor the length is recorded — measure before ordering.

parts.name: M3 × 8 mm countersunk
parts.description: The workhorse M3 screw — used all over the machine wherever a flush head is wanted.

parts.name: M3 × 12 mm countersunk
parts.description: The long M3 countersunk. Used all over the machine.

parts.name: M3 × 20 mm countersunk
parts.description: Joins the C-channel light post to the NEMA bracket. Threads straight into the printed post — no insert, no nut.

parts.name: M3 × 16 mm socket head
parts.description: Bolts the NEMA 17 stepper to the NEMA bracket. 3 per C-channel.

parts.name: M3 × 6 mm button head
parts.description: Holds the layer adapter board down on the chute core's heat inserts.

parts.name: M3 × 12 mm button head
parts.description: Mounts the cable clamp to the interface, and the limit switch to its housing.

parts.name: M3 × 35 mm button head
parts.description: The single long screw that clamps the two cable-clamp halves together.

parts.name: M4 × 6 mm countersunk
parts.description: Fastens the printed PSU housing to the power supply's own case threads.

parts.name: M4 × 12 mm countersunk
parts.description: Bolts each lazy Susan together — 4 a side, through the M4 inserts in the static half and the chute mount. 2 lazy Susans per machine.

parts.name: M5 × 12 mm socket head
parts.description: Holds the NEMA 23 stepper onto the interface bracket.

parts.name: M5 × 16 mm socket head
parts.description: The most-used M5 in the machine: every external bracket, every bin bracket, every interface rib, every lazy Susan extrusion mount.
parts.note: Designed to self-tap into printed plastic wherever it lands in an external bracket or the upper fixed section — no nut and no insert there.

parts.name: M5 × 20 mm socket head
parts.description: Mounts the chute limit switch assembly to the interface frame, into T-nuts.

parts.name: M5 × 30 mm socket head
parts.description: Attaches the cable cage to the six interface brackets.

parts.name: M5 × 35 mm flat head
parts.description: The long screws that stack the cable cage together. Clearly a different screw from the rest of the M5s.

parts.name: M5 socket head — length TBD
parts.description: Placeholder for the three electronics mounts that bolt to the frame. The length has not been measured yet.
parts.note: Length unknown — measure one on the machine before ordering. Six are used: PSU box, control board mount, Orange Pi mount.

parts.name: M3 nut
parts.description: Plain M3 hex nut. Backs the camera-arm screws and the cable clamp's long screw.

parts.name: M5 nut
parts.description: Plain M5 hex nut. Holds the cable cage's six long screws on.

parts.name: Steel rod, 3/8 in — 4 ft stock
parts.description: Bought as a length and cut down. One 4 ft rod yields all four ~1 ft pieces the two overhead camera arms hang from.
parts.note: The metric equivalent is 10 mm rod, and 3/8 in is 9.53 mm — check the printed mount's bore before ordering the metric size.

attributes.label: Buy
attributes.value: One 4 ft (1.22 m) length, or longer

parts.name: Ruthex M3 heat insert (long)
parts.description: ruthex RX-M3x5.7 brass heat-set threaded insert. The default M3 insert anywhere one is called for.
parts.internal_notes: First COTS part in the manifest (unified-parts-system MVP). 'Long' = ruthex's standard M3 (5.7 mm), vs the M3S short (4 mm) used only on the servo adapter. BOM sheet says the machine needs ~235 across machine+layers; the chute core alone takes 18 (4 PCB + 4 bearing race + 4 funnel slides + 4 layer connector + 2 arms).

parts.description: The default M5 insert anywhere one is called for.

parts.note: USB Cables totally vary in quality. Ensure it supports data

parts.name: TMC2209 Stepper Motor Driver Modules
parts.description: Driver for the all steppers

parts.name: Raspberry Pi Pico
parts.description: Embedded MCU for controlling motors

parts.name: 2.54mm header pins
parts.description: Pico Headers

parts.name: Standoff - M3 - 10mm
parts.description: Mount main board
parts.note: Length is the BOM sheet's 10 mm and has not been confirmed against the board mounts.

parts.name: IDC ribbon cable, 16-pin (2×8), 1 m
parts.description: Cable from distribution board to chute

parts.name: IDC ribbon cable, 16-pin (2×8), 30 cm
parts.description: Cable between layer and layer below

parts.name: basically Embedded Control Board
parts.description: In-house distribution/feeder control board: Pico, stepper drivers, servo and LED outputs. 1 per machine.

parts.name: basically Layer Adapter Board
parts.description: In-house adapter board that breaks out the control board's ribbon connectors for one distribution layer. 1 per layer.

parts.name: 50mm LED COB - 24v
parts.description: Lighting for the C-channels and carousel drop zone

parts.name: White/daylight LED strip - 24v
parts.description: Lighting for classification chamber

parts.name: Mechanical Endstop, "Micro Limit Switch Short Hinge Roller Lever Arm Switch for Arduino V-155-1C25"
parts.description: Endstop for chute

parts.name: 24 V → 5 V USB-C buck converter
parts.description: Powers the Orange Pi from the 24 V PSU.

attributes.label: Output
attributes.value: 5 V, 5 A max (25 W) over USB-C

attributes.label: Protection
attributes.value: Reverse, over-voltage, over-current, over-temp, short circuit

parts.name: 24 V PSU, 350 W (Mean Well LRS-350-24)
parts.description: Power supply

vendors.note: sheet's link was broken/truncated; relinked to the LRS-350-24 listing

parts.name: IEC C14 inlet, switch + 10 A fuse
parts.description: Mains inlet for the PSU: switched, fused, pre-wired.

assemblies.name: Light post
assemblies.description: The feeder light post: post, cap, and cap adapter. 2 per machine. Bolts to a C-channel NEMA bracket.

joining.note: The two M3×20 countersunk screws that join the post to the NEMA bracket thread straight into the printed post — no insert, no nut.

assemblies.name: External distribution-frame bracket
assemblies.description: Three parts that bolt together into one external bracket. 6 per distribution frame (per layer), plus a set per interface.

joining.note: All four M5×16 are designed to self-tap: 2 hold the side to the bottom vertical, 2 hold the assembly to the vertical extrusion. No nuts and no inserts anywhere in this bracket.

assemblies.name: Fixed upper section
assemblies.description: The interface's fixed upper section: the upper-fixed-section plate, its ribs (5 plain + 1 with the limit-switch gap), and the big spacer. 1 per interface. Each rib takes 3 M5×16 — 2 into the plate, 1 into the extrusion.

joining.note: 2 of each rib's 3 M5×16 thread straight into the printed upper fixed section — no insert, no nut.
The third M5×16 per rib holds into the aluminum extrusion by friction alone — no T-nut behind it.

assemblies.name: Cable clamp
assemblies.description: Inner + outer ribbon cable clamp; the two halves clamp together around the cable and the pair bolts to the top interface chute mount.

assemblies.name: Classification chamber
assemblies.description: The classification chamber: dome, cap, and finned rotor (lighting parts white).

assemblies.name: Chute bearing assembly
assemblies.description: The flap's bearing assembly: left + right bearing holders, the race between them, and the covers. Carries 10 M3 inserts and the two flap bearings.

assemblies.name: Servo adapter (MG995)
assemblies.description: MG995 servo adapter: servo side + flap side.

assemblies.name: Servo assembly (MG995)
assemblies.description: The MG995 servo in its bracket: lower arm, side arm, cover, housing. Bolts to the chute core's two arm inserts as a unit.

assemblies.name: Overhead camera mount
assemblies.description: Camera mount for the C-channels. 2 per machine, each hanging off two ~1 ft steel rods.

assemblies.name: Layer connector
assemblies.description: Pair of connectors. 1 of each per layer + 1 of each per bottom interface.

assemblies.name: Chute limit switch
assemblies.description: Interface limit switch: printed dowel pin, housing, hammer, and the switch itself. Bolts to the interface frame as a unit.

assemblies.name: Cable cage
assemblies.description: Six cable cage brackets (5 regular + 1 cable mount) stacked with the ribbon cable clamp. Also sandwiches the laser-cut cage top and bottom plates, which are not in the parts registry yet.

assemblies.name: Meanwell 24V PSU box
assemblies.description: Enclosure for the Meanwell 24V power supply: back mount, connections plate, and cap, screwed to the supply's own case.

assemblies.name: Camera extension
assemblies.description: Classification-channel camera extension for the 4K camera module: the 50 mm extension tube, its mount, and the camera. 1 per machine.

assemblies.name: Pico with headers
assemblies.description: The Raspberry Pi Pico with 2.54 mm header pins fitted, so it can seat in the PCB. The board ships bare — the pins are a separate purchase.

joining.note: Every pin solders into the Pico's through-holes. Nothing here presses or clips together.

assemblies.name: Sorter V2
assemblies.description: The whole machine: feeder on top, interface, the distribution layers between the interfaces, and the bottom interface.

assemblies.name: Feeder
assemblies.description: Feeder + classification channel. Everything above the interface.

assemblies.name: Interface
assemblies.description: The interface between the feeder and the distribution layers. Its lazy Susan sandwiches the upper fixed section and the chute mount, 4 M4 screws a side.

assemblies.name: Distribution layer
assemblies.description: One distribution layer between the interfaces: frame, chute, bin brackets, funnels, bins. Each bin bracket takes 2 M5×16 into a T-nut.

assemblies.name: Bottom interface
assemblies.description: The bottom interface / lazy Susan level. Carries two chutes.

assemblies.name: Chute
assemblies.description: One chute: the core plus its door module, funnel brackets, connectors, and PCB. Every screw here lands in one of the chute core's 18 M3 inserts.

assemblies.name: Flap module
assemblies.description: The chute's flap: the door itself, the bearing assembly it swings on, and the servo that drives it.

assemblies.name: Chute PCB
assemblies.description: The layer adapter board, screwed onto the chute core's four PCB inserts.

assemblies.name: C-channel drive
assemblies.description: One C-channel drive unit: stator, NEMA bracket, output gear, gear train and stepper. 4 per machine — 3 in the feeder plus the classification channel's. The rotor differs by location (faceted in the feeder, finned in the classification chamber), so it is not part of this node.

assemblies.name: Interface NEMA 23 mount
assemblies.description: The NEMA 23 stepper on its interface bracket. 1 per interface.

assemblies.name: Interface spur gear drive
assemblies.description: The interface spur gear with its 8 mm bore timing pulley. 1 per interface.

assemblies.name: Interface chute gear + mount
assemblies.description: The chute gear screwed down onto the top interface chute mount — two sections of the split spur gear that become one turning unit.

assemblies.name: Distribution frame
assemblies.description: The frame of one distribution layer: six external brackets, the crossbeams between them, and the 90° brackets.

assemblies.name: Control board mount
assemblies.description: The basically Embedded Control Board standing off its bracket. The printed bracket is not in the parts registry yet.

assemblies.name: Orange Pi mount
assemblies.description: The Orange Pi 5 standing off its bracket. The printed bracket is not in the parts registry yet.

assemblies.name: Electronics
assemblies.description: The three boxes that bolt to the frame: the PSU, the control board, and the Orange Pi. Each takes 2 M5 socket heads of a length nobody has measured yet.

assemblies.name: Interface bracket
assemblies.description: One interface bracket with its spacer, bolted onto its vertical extrusion. 6 per interface.

assemblies.name: Bottom lazy Susan
assemblies.description: The bottom interface's lazy Susan: the static half, the chute mount that spins on it, the extrusion mounts, and the bearing itself. Bolted through 4 M4 screws a side, same as the top interface's.
